last_item: Band-2.5 (resource & config safety): host-sized tuning — pure RecommendTuning sizing function (shared_buffers/effective_cache_size/work_mem/maintenance_work_mem/max_connections from RAM/CPU, selectable workload profile, default mixed), surfaced (not applied) in Provision.
last_result: done (iter 28) — added pure RecommendTuning(memoryMB, cpuCount, profile) in internal/pg/tuning.go faithfully porting the sm tuning.py math (DEFAULTS.md): shared_buffers pct oltp .25/mixed .30/olap .40; effective_cache_size 75% RAM; work_mem oltp 64/mixed 128/olap clamp(RAM/32,128,1024); maintenance_work_mem olap min(RAM/4,4096)/else min(RAM/8,2048); max_connections = clamp(int(RAM*(1-pct)*0.5/perConn), profile bounds). WorkloadProfile + ParseWorkloadProfile (empty->Mixed default, unknown->ValidationError so a typo can't silently mis-size). Total/panic-free on degenerate input (neg/zero RAM->0, cpu<1->1, unknown profile->Mixed). NOT dead code: wired into Provision via detectHostTuning (readMemInfo + runtime.NumCPU, 4GB fallback like sm) to SURFACE the recommendation in the result (recommended_tuning Data map + 'not yet applied' step note) — best-defaults transparency, informational ONLY, computing it never touches PG so it cannot fail provisioning. Acceptance met: TestRecommendTuning_SampleRAMValues table test (1GB-128GB x oltp/olap/mixed, hand-computed expecteds) + profile-ordering + floor/ceil clamp + degenerate-input + ParseWorkloadProfile + SettingsMap tests. Go gate green (gofmt/vet/test/build, sandbox-disabled per snap); web untouched. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): math faithful, no blocking issues; addressed one cosmetic comment-clarity nit.
notes: BAND 1.5 + BAND 2 COMPLETE. IN BAND 2.5 (resource & config safety). DONE iter 28: host-sized tuning PURE SIZING FUNCTION — RecommendTuning(memoryMB, cpuCount, profile) in internal/pg/tuning.go ports the sm tuning.py math (DEFAULTS.md) for shared_buffers/effective_cache_size/work_mem/maintenance_work_mem/max_connections; WorkloadProfile selectable, empty->Mixed default, unknown->ValidationError; total/panic-free on degenerate input. Surfaced (NOT applied) in Provision via detectHostTuning (readMemInfo + runtime.NumCPU, 4GB fallback) as recommended_tuning Data + 'not yet applied' step — informational best-defaults transparency, can't fail provisioning. Tested vs sample RAM 1GB-128GB x all 3 profiles + clamp/ordering/degenerate/parse/SettingsMap. THE NEW TOP BAND-2.5 ITEM is the APPLY follow-up: persist these via ALTER SYSTEM and restart through restartWithRollback (iter-26 self-healing primitive) — snapshot postgresql.auto.conf before restart-requiring shared_buffers/max_connections, pg_reload_conf for reloadable ones; needs a real-PG integration test (settings land in pg_settings; a deliberately-bad value rolls back with PG up) + UI showing applied defaults & the labeled profile override. That apply MUST funnel through restartWithRollback. DONE iter 27: connection-saturation alert CRITICAL tier connections-critical (pg.connections_percent >= 95%, For 1m, Cooldown 15m, Critical) escalating the existing connections-near-max WARNING (85%); rule-only, metric already real (sampler.go pg_stat_activity / max_connections), ID-based seed auto-adds on upgrade; TestConnectionSaturationTiers proves both tiers fire non-vacuously. DONE iter 26: SELF-HEALING config primitive (internal/pg/safeconfig.go): snapshotAutoConf captures <data_directory>/postgresql.auto.conf BEFORE a restart-requiring change (fails closed if unreadable); restartWithRollback restarts PG and on non-zero `systemctl restart` exit restores the snapshot via `tee` as the postgres user (keeps owner+0600; works while PG is down, unlike ALTER SYSTEM RESET) + restarts again so PG is never left down -> CodeSafety ('rolled back; PG running') or CodeInternal ('PG down'+hint). ANY future config-apply/override flow MUST funnel its restart through restartWithRollback (snapshot before write) — this is exactly what the new APPLY item above must do. DONE iter 25: disk-headroom-low early-warning rule (WARNING 80% host.disk_percent) below critical disk-almost-full (90%). KEY: adding a disk/conn alert is usually RULE-ONLY — Snapshot already has the fields, alert/metrics.go metricValue computes the percent, and background.go's seedDefaultAlertRules inserts only missing IDs (idempotent) so a new DefaultRules() entry auto-seeds on upgrade. Test pattern: extend TestDefaultRules want-list + a focused engine test; background_test asserts len(DefaultRules()) dynamically. --- Alert plumbing: Collector.SampleOnce -> Engine.Evaluate -> dispatchAlertEvents (background.go); metricValue (alert/metrics.go) reads Snapshot fields; go is a snap -> run go/psql/pg_ctl sandbox-disabled; empty $TMPDIR in some shells -> use /tmp. --- HISTORICAL: band 1.5 (backups proven-restorable, S3 nudge, restore-test verify+deep, scheduled jobs) and band 2 (web silent-failure audit, idempotent provisioning, query-box auto-LIMIT+statement_timeout enforced) all COMPLETE. See git log.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
